# Issues Pivot

---

## [PROF-01] Instrument the ingestion path with phase timings
**Status:** DONE
**Files:** `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/ui/index.ts`, `src/ts/ui/stats.ts`
**Labels:** p1, perf, profiling, pivot
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:30:41.0787558Z
**Closed:** 2026-05-21T17:22:49.58638Z

Context: Capture an honest baseline before moving work into a worker or Rust.

**Concrete change:** expand the existing performance instrumentation into one consistent import timeline covering enumeration, virtual handle creation, metadata indexing, tree-model build, selection-index build, DOM render, report generation, visible-ready, and export without changing the product architecture.
**Main surface:** `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/ui/index.ts`, `src/ts/ui/stats.ts`
**Proof of done:** a large import run emits stable timing output plus counts for files, folders, ignored entries, rendered rows, selected files, represented bytes, and exported bytes so later issues can compare regressions and wins with context.
**Out of scope:** workerization, Rust/WASM integration, lazy-content refactors, virtualization, and hosting changes.

**Resolution:** **What changed:** Added phase-level `performance.mark`/`measure` instrumentation and `[counts]` log lines across `app.ts`, `filesystem.ts`, and `ui/index.ts`. Added `ignoredCount` field to `ScanAggregator` to track IGNORE_LIST skips during directory scan.

**Why:** Captures an honest baseline timeline — build-handle, scan (enumeration+metadata+tree-model), init-tree-state (selection-index), render-tree (DOM), refresh-ui, report-generate, visible-load, export-zip, export-combined — plus stable counts (files, folders, ignored, rendered-rows, selected, bytes, export-bytes) so later worker/Rust issues can measure regressions and wins against a known baseline.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exits clean. `neti check` → PASS WITH FINDINGS. Dead-code findings on changed files: `dragCounter` in `app.ts` is pre-existing and explicitly live in the drag overlay flow (per briefing); excluded from removal. All other findings are outside the working set.

**Handoff:** Baseline timing output now appears in DevTools console on every import. Next issue: compare these numbers after workerization or Rust/WASM integration lands.


---

## [WORK-01] Define the metadata worker contract
**Status:** DONE
**Files:** `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/types/index.ts`, `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts`
**Labels:** p1, architecture, worker, pivot
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:30:41.0787705Z
**Closed:** 2026-05-21T17:26:34.7005713Z
**Depends on:** [PROF-01]

Context: The pivot needs a stable background boundary before any compute moves off the main thread.

**Concrete change:** define the request and response contract for background metadata ingestion, add the worker scaffold, and wire the app to initialize the worker while keeping browser-owned file handles out of worker messages.
**Main surface:** `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/types/index.ts`, `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts`
**Proof of done:** the app boots with the worker in place, the message schema is explicit, worker messages are batch-oriented and serializable, and stubbed metadata responses can round-trip without posting `VirtualFileHandle` or `VirtualDirectoryHandle`.
**Out of scope:** moving real scan/index work into the worker, Rust/WASM loading, lazy-content behavior, and tree virtualization.

**Resolution:** **What changed:** Added serializable worker message types (`SerializableEntry`, `WorkerInboundMessage`, `WorkerOutboundMessage`) to `types/index.ts`. Created `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts` with a stub ping/scan-batch handler. Wired `initWorker()` into `app.ts` startup — worker initializes on boot, logs ready on pong.

**Why:** Establishes the boundary that prevents browser file handles from crossing into the worker thread. All message types are structurally cloneable. The stub round-trip proves the contract works before any real computation moves off the main thread.

**Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exits clean. `neti check` → PASS WITH FINDINGS. Dead-code candidates on changed/working-set files: none. All findings are outside the working set (tree.ts, pretext.ts, modals.ts, global.d.ts, fs_utils.ts) — pre-existing, no action required.

**Handoff:** Next issue should wire real scan metadata batches from `finishScan`/`processFileList` into the worker via `scan-batch` messages, keeping file handles on the main thread.

**Files:** `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/ui/index.ts`, `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts`

**Depends on:** [WORK-01], [DATA-01], [DATA-02]

Context: Once the worker contract exists, the first performance win is moving metadata-heavy indexing off the main thread without changing languages yet.

**Concrete change:** shift metadata normalization, tree construction, and stats preparation into the TypeScript worker while preserving the current browser-side file enumeration flow.
**Main surface:** `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/ui/index.ts`, `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts`
**Proof of done:** large imports keep the UI responsive while tree and stats data arrive from the worker with behavior parity on representative repositories.
**Out of scope:** Rust/WASM integration, lazy full-text reads, export chunking, and tree virtualization.

## [DATA-01] Make initial import metadata-first and defer file reads
**Status:** ACTIVE
**Files:** `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/utils/crossbrowser_fs.ts`, `src/ts/ui/viewer.ts`, `src/ts/features.ts`
**Labels:** p1, perf, ingestion, lazy-content
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:30:41.0787859Z
**Depends on:** [PROF-01]

Context: The pivot only works at Kubernetes scale if initial import stops meaning "read every file body immediately."

**Concrete change:** audit and enforce a metadata-only initial import so the first pass builds tree and stats state without content reads, while viewer and export continue to fetch file bodies only when explicitly needed.
**Main surface:** `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/utils/crossbrowser_fs.ts`, `src/ts/ui/viewer.ts`, `src/ts/features.ts`
**Proof of done:** during initial import no `File.text()`, `Blob.text()`, `arrayBuffer()`, or sniff-based content reads occur; only metadata needed for tree, stats, and indexing is collected, and viewer/export remain the approved content-read paths.
**Out of scope:** worker migration, Rust compute migration, tree virtualization, and chunked export assembly.

**Resolution:**

## [UX-01] Virtualize tree rendering for very large repositories
**Status:** BACKLOG

**Labels:** p1, ux, perf, tree
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:30:41.0788011Z
**Depends on:** [PROF-01]

**Proof of done:** very large trees render through a virtualized row window while scroll, expand/collapse, and selection interactions remain correct.
**Out of scope:** moving more logic into Rust, lazy-content export changes, and hosting work.

## [EXPO-01] Generate report manifests and assemble exports in chunks
**Status:** BACKLOG
**Files:** `src/ts/features.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/state.ts`
**Labels:** p1, export, perf, memory
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:30:41.0788079Z
**Depends on:** [DATA-01]

Context: Large exports should not depend on one giant concatenated string in memory.

**Concrete change:** generate a selected-file report manifest first, then assemble the combined export from BlobPart chunks instead of repeated giant string concatenation.
**Main surface:** `src/ts/features.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/state.ts`
**Proof of done:** the combined export path uses manifest-driven chunk assembly, limits reads to selected or included files, reports progress during long runs, and still produces the expected output for selected files.
**Out of scope:** zip packaging redesign, Rust-owned selection state, and hosting changes.

**Resolution:**

## [OPS-01] Deploy the app to GitHub Pages with a custom-domain-ready workflow
**Status:** DONE
**Files:** `.github/workflows/pages.yml`, `vite.config.ts`, `src/ts/app.ts`, `README.md`
**Labels:** p1, ops, deployment, github-pages
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:30:41.0788131Z
**Closed:** 2026-05-21T16:49:20.3841558Z

Context: Hosting should move off Vercel and onto a GitHub-only static deployment path.

**Concrete change:** add the GitHub Pages workflow, make the Vite and runtime asset-path assumptions explicit for Pages hosting, and document the GitHub Pages plus custom-domain handoff steps.
**Main surface:** `.github/workflows/pages.yml`, `vite.config.ts`, `src/ts/app.ts`, `README.md`
**Proof of done:** pushes on `main` can build and deploy the static app through GitHub Pages, the app resolves its filetypes data through a Pages-safe base path, and the repo documents the required Pages, DNS, and HTTPS setup steps.
**Out of scope:** buying the domain, performing the final DNS cutover, and compute-architecture changes.

**Resolution:** **What changed:** Added a GitHub Pages Actions workflow for `main`, switched Vite to a relative asset base for Pages-safe output, updated the filetypes JSON fetch to resolve relative to the deployed page URL, and documented the Pages plus custom-domain setup flow in the README.

**Why:** The app needed a GitHub-only deployment path that works on both the default GitHub Pages URL and a future custom domain without relying on Vercel or repo-specific path assumptions.

**Verification:** `npm run build` passed and produced `dist/index.html` with relative asset URLs (`./assets/...`). `neti check` completed successfully; it reported a dead-code candidate on `dragCounter` in `src/ts/app.ts`, but direct inspection showed that variable is actively read and written in the drag overlay flow, so it was treated as a false positive rather than changing live behavior.

**Handoff:** In GitHub, set `Settings -> Pages -> Source` to `GitHub Actions`, push `main`, and then add the custom domain plus DNS records described in `README.md` before enabling `Enforce HTTPS`.

## [DATA-02] Split browser handles from transferable metadata
**Status:** BACKLOG
**Files:** `src/ts/types/index.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts`
**Labels:** p1, architecture, worker, metadata, pivot
**Recorded:** 2026-05-21T16:40:52.3224366Z
**Depends on:** [WORK-01]

Context: The worker and Rust boundaries must not receive browser file handles. Browser-owned handles should remain in TypeScript state while background compute receives only serializable metadata.

**Concrete change:** split browser file handles from transferable file metadata by introducing stable file IDs and a serializable metadata record used by worker messages.
**Main surface:** `src/ts/types/index.ts`, `src/ts/filesystem.ts`, `src/ts/app.ts`, `src/ts/workers/scan.worker.ts`
**Proof of done:** worker-facing messages contain file IDs and serializable metadata only, while `VirtualFileHandle` and `VirtualDirectoryHandle` remain in main-thread browser state and are not posted to the worker.
**Out of scope:** moving real indexing into the worker, Rust/WASM integration, lazy content reads, tree virtualization, and export redesign.

**Resolution:**
